annotations.label: General relativity

anchor.snippet: General relativity is a theory of gravitation developed by Albert Einstein between 1907 and 1915

annotations.note: General relativity as a physical theory of gravitation is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Equivalence principle

anchor.snippet: the principle states that a person in a free-falling elevator cannot tell that they are in free fall

annotations.note: A physical principle with no Mathlib formalization.

annotations.label: Indistinguishability of gravity and acceleration

anchor.snippet: An observer in a closed room cannot tell which of the following two scenarios is true

annotations.note: Physical thought experiment, not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Gravitational force proportional to mass

anchor.snippet: an object in a gravitational field should feel a gravitational force proportional to its mass

annotations.note: Physics statement about gravitational force, not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Gravitational frequency shift of light

anchor.snippet: The first new effect is the gravitational frequency shift of light

annotations.note: Physical prediction with no Mathlib counterpart.

annotations.label: Gravitational time dilation

anchor.snippet: time runs more slowly for observers the lower they are in a gravitational field

annotations.note: Relativistic time dilation is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Gravitational deflection of light

anchor.snippet: Einstein predicted the gravitational deflection of light

annotations.note: Physical prediction absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Tidal effect

anchor.snippet: responsible for the tides in the Earth's oceans

annotations.note: Tidal/geodesic-deviation physics is not formalized.

annotations.label: Minkowski space

anchor.snippet: introduced Minkowski space , a geometric formulation of Einstein's special theory of relativity

annotations.note: Mathlib's 'Minkowski' refers to geometry-of-numbers and inequalities, not the Lorentzian spacetime of special relativity.

annotations.label: Wheeler summary of gravity

anchor.snippet: spacetime tells matter how to move; matter tells spacetime how to curve

annotations.note: An informal physical aphorism, not a Mathlib statement.

annotations.label: Test particle

anchor.snippet: particles that are influenced by gravity, but are so small and light that we can neglect their own gravitational effect

annotations.note: A physics modeling notion with no Mathlib formalization.

annotations.label: Geodesic (spacetime)

anchor.snippet: test particles move along lines called geodesics

annotations.note: Mathlib has no differential-geometric geodesics (only graph/quiver 'geodesics'), and no Lorentzian geodesics.

annotations.label: Geodesic on Earth's surface

anchor.snippet: a geodesic is the shortest route between two points on the Earth's surface

annotations.note: Shortest paths/great circles on a surface as geodesics are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Mass–energy equivalence

anchor.snippet: The equivalence between mass and energy

annotations.note: E=mc² is physics with no Mathlib formalization.

annotations.label: Stress–energy tensor as source

anchor.snippet: a more general physical quantity called the stress–energy tensor

annotations.note: The stress–energy tensor is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Metric

anchor.snippet: described by a quantity called a metric

annotations.note: Mathlib has Riemannian (positive-definite) metrics on manifolds via RiemannianBundle/IsRiemannianManifold, but not the indefinite Lorentzian spacetime metric of GR.

annotations.label: Riemann curvature tensor

anchor.snippet: a geometrical quantity called the Riemann curvature tensor

annotations.note: No Riemann/sectional curvature tensor is defined in Mathlib's manifold library.

annotations.label: Einstein tensor

anchor.snippet: now called the Einstein tensor

annotations.note: The Einstein tensor is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Einstein field equation

anchor.snippet: Einstein's equation then states that

annotations.note: The Einstein field equations are not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Schwarzschild solution

anchor.snippet: the Schwarzschild solution describes the geometry around a spherical, non-rotating mass

annotations.note: No Schwarzschild metric/solution exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Kerr solution

anchor.snippet: the Kerr solution describes a rotating black hole

annotations.note: No Kerr solution is formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Friedmann–Lemaître–Robertson–Walker solution

anchor.snippet: Friedmann–Lemaître–Robertson–Walker solution

annotations.note: No FLRW cosmological solution exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Minkowski spacetime (simplest solution)

anchor.snippet: The simplest solution is the uncurved Minkowski spacetime

annotations.note: Flat Minkowski spacetime as a solution of Einstein's equations is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Anomalous perihelion shift

anchor.snippet: this deviation from Newton's orbits is known as the anomalous perihelion shift

annotations.note: An experimental relativistic prediction not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Deflection of starlight by the Sun

anchor.snippet: starlight is deflected as it passes near the Sun, leading to apparent shifts of up to 1.75 arc seconds

annotations.note: Physical light-deflection prediction with no Mathlib counterpart.

annotations.label: Gravitational redshift measurement

anchor.snippet: Gravitational redshift was first measured in a laboratory setting in 1959

annotations.note: An experimental result not represented in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Shapiro time delay

anchor.snippet: precision measurements of the Shapiro effect or gravitational time delay for light

annotations.note: The Shapiro time delay is physics, not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Geodetic and frame-dragging effects

anchor.snippet: The geodetic and frame-dragging effects were both tested by the Gravity Probe B satellite experiment

annotations.note: Relativistic precession effects are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Gravitational lensing

anchor.snippet: the corresponding gravitational effect is called gravitational lensing

annotations.note: Gravitational lensing is a physical effect absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Gravitational waves

anchor.snippet: are distortions of geometry that propagate at the speed of light

annotations.note: Gravitational waves are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Binary pulsar PSR1913+16

anchor.snippet: this energy loss was observed in 1974 in a binary pulsar called PSR1913+16

annotations.note: An astrophysical observation, not a Mathlib object.

annotations.label: Black hole

anchor.snippet: a region of space with a gravitational effect so strong that not even light can escape

annotations.note: Black holes are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Black-hole uniqueness (no-hair) theorems

anchor.snippet: what are called black-hole uniqueness theorems

annotations.note: No-hair/uniqueness theorems for black holes are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Homogeneous and isotropic universe

anchor.snippet: the universe is approximately homogeneous and isotropic

annotations.note: A cosmological modeling assumption with no Mathlib formalization.

annotations.label: Cosmological constant

anchor.snippet: adding a term called the cosmological constant

annotations.note: The cosmological constant is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Singularity theorems

anchor.snippet: there are so-called singularity theorems which predict that such singularities must exist within the universe

annotations.note: The Penrose–Hawking singularity theorems are not formalized in Mathlib.
